text.title: Project file layout and bootstrap contract
text.summary: The workflow's contract is a fixed on-disk layout under the project's `.product/` folder. Master-prompt + state + decisions + tasks + research are the five durable artifacts. Every subagent reads its inputs from disk; the orchestrator never pastes long prompts.

section.title: Directory layout
section.p: Every project driven by this workflow has a `.product/` folder at its root with this shape.
section.code: .product/ ├── master-prompt.md # WHAT we build, WHY, conventions, /faion resources ├── state.md # CURRENT phase, in-flight tasks, one-line log ├── decisions/decisions.md # append-only architectural decisions ├── tasks/NN-<slug>.md # per-task briefs read by subagents ├── research/NN-<slug>.md # subagent outputs land here BEFORE summary └── (optional) features/NN-<slug>/ # SDD feature folders once Phase 3 lands
section.p: The folder names and the file shape are the contract. Project content (vision, stack, billing model) is project-specific; the file layout is not.

section.title: master-prompt.md
section.p: Single entry point. Every agent (orchestrator and every subagent) reads it first. Holds the project vision, architecture sketch, phase list, conventions inherited from the workspace, and the list of `/faion` resources to invoke per phase. It is rewritten only when the vision itself changes; phase progression does not edit it.

section.title: state.md
section.p: Refreshed every cron tick. Holds: current phase, in-flight subagents, last action, next action, parked open questions. The log section is one line per tick — symbols not paragraphs.
section.reference: Tick semantics and the log format.

rule.statement: Architectural decisions MUST be appended to `decisions/decisions.md` with: ISO date, decision (one sentence), rationale (why this beats alternatives), alternatives considered (list with rejection reasons). Past entries are NEVER rewritten.
rule.rationale: External callers (commits, SDD tickets, code review) cite decisions by date. Rewriting silently breaks citations. Append-only plus a chosen-vs-rejected pair makes the trail trustworthy across compactions and restarts.

section.title: tasks/ and research/
section.p: Each task brief under `tasks/NN-<slug>.md` is self-contained: goal, required output structure, `/faion` resources to use, search tool chain, constraints, return-summary word cap. The matching subagent saves its full output to `research/NN-<slug>.md` before returning the bounded summary to the orchestrator.

rule.statement: Subagents MUST save full output to `research/NN-<slug>.md` BEFORE returning a summary. The orchestrator's summary read is bounded; full output is not re-read unless an architectural decision depends on a specific quote.
rule.rationale: If the summary alone could contain the full output, the orchestrator carries N copies of it across ticks. Persisting on disk keeps the orchestrator lean and lets future ticks re-source detail without re-running the subagent.

section.title: Bootstrap step
section.p: On first invocation, the orchestrator creates `.product/` with empty `state.md` (phase 1 pending) and a fresh `master-prompt.md` derived from the operator's one-line ask. The first task briefs (one per Phase 1 research dimension) are written before any subagent dispatch. After bootstrap, the cron loop takes over.

rule.statement: Every directory created by this workflow MUST get an `AGENTS.md` (essential context, file table) and a `CLAUDE.md` (`@AGENTS.md` only). This includes `.product/`, `.product/tasks/`, `.product/research/`, and any `features/<id>/` folder.
rule.rationale: The workspace convention is that any directory without the pair is invisible to future agents. Skipping the pair means later sessions cannot route through the project.
